goal: Introduce plugin architecture with NorbertAPI contract, flexible two-zone layout engine with floating panels, multi-window support, sidebar customization, and validate via norbert-session first-party plugin migration.

phases.name: Plugin Host Foundation (M1)
phases.description: Plugin loading pipeline, NorbertAPI contract, sandbox, dependency resolution.

steps.name: Plugin types and NorbertAPI contract definition
steps.description: Define algebraic data types for PluginManifest, NorbertPlugin interface, NorbertAPI with all 7 sub-APIs, ViewRegistration, TabRegistration, and resolution error types.
acceptance_criteria: NorbertAPI type provides db, hooks, ui, mcp, events, config, plugins sub-APIs
PluginManifest type captures id, name, version, norbert_api, dependencies
ViewRegistration type captures id, pluginId, label, icon, primaryView, minWidth, minHeight, floatMetric
scenarios: NorbertAPI provides all required sub-APIs

steps.name: Walking skeleton: plugin loads, registers view, appears in UI
steps.description: Implement PluginLoader scan pipeline, NorbertAPI Factory, Plugin Registry, and Lifecycle Manager so a test plugin can register a view discoverable in the view picker and sidebar.
acceptance_criteria: Plugin loader scans and finds installed plugin packages
NorbertAPI Factory creates scoped API instance per plugin
Registered view appears in view picker grouped under plugin name
Sidebar icon appears for the registering plugin
Clicking sidebar icon assigns plugin's primaryView to Main zone
scenarios: Plugin registers a view and user can access it (walking skeleton)
Plugin registers view via api.ui.registerView()

steps.name: Plugin sandbox enforcement
steps.description: Enforce namespace scoping on plugin db access. Core tables read-only for plugins. Plugin writes restricted to plugin_{id}_* tables.
acceptance_criteria: Plugin db writes scoped to plugin_{pluginId}_* tables succeed
Write to core 'sessions' table rejected with explicit namespace error
Plugin with invalid manifest rejected at load time with missing field identified
scenarios: Plugin sandbox prevents writes to core tables
Plugin can only write to its own namespaced tables
Plugin with invalid manifest is rejected at load time

steps.name: Hook processor registration and status bar API
steps.description: Implement api.hooks.register() for event delivery to plugins and api.ui.registerStatusItem() for dynamic status bar items.
acceptance_criteria: Registered hook processor receives hook events after registration
Plugin can write derived data to namespaced tables from hook handler
Status bar item appears and updates dynamically
scenarios: Plugin registers hook processor via api.hooks
Plugin registers status bar item via api.ui

steps.name: Inter-plugin API composition
steps.description: Implement api.plugins.get() for declared dependency API access. Undeclared dependencies rejected.
acceptance_criteria: api.plugins.get() returns dependency's public API for declared dependencies
api.plugins.get() for undeclared dependency returns error
scenarios: Plugin accesses dependency public API via api.plugins
Plugin cannot access undeclared dependency API

steps.name: Dependency resolver with topological sort and semver
steps.description: Resolve plugin dependency graph via topological sort with semver range validation. Handle missing, disabled, circular, and version-mismatch cases.
acceptance_criteria: Dependent plugin loads after its dependency
Startup reports correct plugin and view counts
Missing dependency prevents load with actionable error listing what to install
Version mismatch is a hard failure with specific version in error
Circular dependency detected and reported
scenarios: Plugins load in topological dependency order
All dependencies satisfied results in clean startup
Missing dependency prevents plugin load with actionable error
Version mismatch is a hard failure
Circular dependency is detected and reported
Multiple missing dependencies listed in single error

steps.name: Disabled dependency degradation and runtime disable
steps.description: Handle disabled dependencies with degradation warnings, greyed-out placeholders with re-enable action, and runtime disable/enable without crash.
acceptance_criteria: Disabled dependency triggers per-feature degradation warning with re-enable action
Greyed-out placeholders include one-click re-enable path
Runtime dependency disable triggers graceful degradation without crash
scenarios: Disabled dependency triggers degradation warning
Runtime dependency disable triggers graceful degradation
Greyed-out placeholders include one-click re-enable path

phases.name: Layout Engine and Persistence (M2)
phases.description: Two-zone model, divider, view assignment, floating panels, persistence, presets.

steps.name: Layout state types and zone registry
steps.description: Define algebraic data types for LayoutState, ZoneState, PresetState, FloatingPanelState. Implement zone registry as named Map with Main always present.
acceptance_criteria: Zone registry stores zones as keyed map, not positional fields
Main zone is always present and cannot be removed
Zone abstraction is count-agnostic
scenarios: Main zone is always present and shows content
Zone registry is count-agnostic (@property)

steps.name: Walking skeleton: layout persists across restart
steps.description: Implement Layout Persistor with auto-save (debounced) and restore. Atomic write-then-rename pattern for crash safety. Validate view IDs against plugin registry on restore.
acceptance_criteria: Layout with zone assignments and divider position survives restart
Auto-save triggers on every layout change without manual action
Divider position persisted as 0.0-1.0 ratio
Invalid view IDs on restore show graceful empty state
Layout save-restore roundtrip preserves all state (@property)
scenarios: User arranges workspace and arrangement survives restart (walking skeleton)
Layout auto-saves on every change
Layout save-restore roundtrip preserves all state (@property)
Layout restore validates view IDs against current plugin registry

steps.name: Secondary zone toggle and divider
steps.description: Implement Secondary zone toggle, draggable divider with 280px minimum constraint, double-click 50/50 snap, and zone renderer mounting views into zones.
acceptance_criteria: Secondary zone toggleable; hiding collapses to full-width Main
Reshowing Secondary restores last-used view
Divider drag resizes both zones with 280px minimum enforced
Double-click divider snaps to 50/50
First launch with no layout file shows empty Main with guidance
scenarios: Secondary zone opens with view assignment
Toggle Secondary zone hides and restores (both sub-scenarios)
Dragging the divider resizes both zones
Double-click divider snaps to 50/50 split
Minimum zone width is enforced at 280px (both directions)
First launch with no layout file shows empty Main with guidance
Divider position stored as ratio for resolution independence

steps.name: View assignment engine (right-click, drag, picker)
steps.description: Unify four assignment mechanisms into single state mutation. Context menu generated from zone registry. Drag with drop overlay. Searchable view picker via command palette.
acceptance_criteria: Right-click context menu offers zone targets generated from registry
Drag from sidebar to zone shows branded overlay and assigns on drop
View picker shows searchable list grouped by plugin
All four mechanisms produce identical layout state (@property)
Assigning a view replaces current zone occupant
scenarios: Right-click context menu assigns view to zone
Drag sidebar icon to zone with visual feedback
View picker assigns view via keyboard search
All assignment mechanisms produce identical zone state (@property)
Assigning a view replaces the current zone occupant
Drag to invalid drop area has no effect
Context menu items generated dynamically from zone registry
Assigning unregistered view ID shows graceful empty state

steps.name: Floating panels with pill minimize
steps.description: Implement floating panel lifecycle: open, resize, reposition, snap-to-edge, minimize to pill with live floatMetric, restore, switch mode, close. Multiple panels simultaneously.
acceptance_criteria: Any registered view opens as resizable, repositionable floating panel
Panel snaps to window edge within 20px
Minimize collapses to pill with view name and live floatMetric
Pill click restores panel to previous size and position
Multiple floating panels coexist independently
scenarios: Any registered view can open as a floating panel
Floating panel snaps to window edges
Minimize to pill with live metric
Clicking pill restores panel to previous size and position
Floating panel Switch Mode via menu
Multiple floating panels can be open simultaneously
View without floatMetric minimizes to pill with name only
Floating panel position and size persist across restarts
Closing floating panel removes it from layout state

steps.name: Named presets with Layout Picker
steps.description: Implement Layout Picker (Ctrl+Shift+L) for save/recall of named presets. Built-in presets immutable. Custom presets support create, rename, delete. Reset to Default escape hatch.
acceptance_criteria: Named preset saved and appears in Layout Picker
Selecting preset instantly changes layout
Built-in presets cannot be deleted; Save Copy available
Reset to Default restores single Main zone
Preset referencing uninstalled plugin shows graceful empty state
scenarios: Save named layout preset
Switch between layout presets
Built-in presets cannot be deleted
Reset to Default layout
Preset referencing uninstalled plugin shows graceful empty state
Custom presets can be renamed and deleted

phases.name: Multi-Window Support
phases.description: Independent windows sharing single backend, per-window layout, window labels.

steps.name: Window factory and IPC router
steps.description: Implement Tauri window creation from multiple entry points (right-click, Ctrl+Shift+N, tray, command palette). IPC event subscription per window. Single backend process invariant.
acceptance_criteria: New window opens via right-click 'Open in New Window' with selected view
New window opens via keyboard shortcut with default layout
Both windows receive live hook event updates
Single backend process regardless of window count
scenarios: Open new window via right-click on sidebar icon
Open new window via keyboard shortcut
Both windows receive live event updates
Single backend process regardless of window count

steps.name: Per-window layout independence and persistence
steps.description: Each window gets independent Layout Engine instance. Per-window layout files. Window labels in title and status bar. Window set persisted for restart restore.
acceptance_criteria: Two windows maintain fully independent layouts
Per-window layout persists as layout-{window-id}.json
All open windows reopen on next launch with saved layouts
Window label appears in title bar and status bar
Closing one window does not affect others
scenarios: Two windows with independent layouts
Per-window layout persists across restart
Label a window for identification
Closing one window does not affect others
Last window closing keeps backend alive in tray mode
No performance degradation with two windows (@property)

phases.name: Sidebar Customization
phases.description: Sidebar icon visibility toggle, drag-to-reorder, persistence, command palette fallback.

steps.name: Sidebar visibility, reorder, and persistence
steps.description: Right-click toggle list with checkmarks. Drag-to-reorder for core and plugin sections. Persist to sidebar.json. Hidden sections accessible via command palette. Reset to defaults. New plugins append at end.
acceptance_criteria: Right-click shows full section toggle list; unchecking hides icon
Drag-to-reorder changes display order and persists
Hidden sections accessible via command palette without changing visibility
Reset Sidebar restores default visibility and order
Newly installed plugin icon appends at end without disrupting order
scenarios: Toggle sidebar icon visibility via right-click
Right-click shows full section toggle list with checkmarks
Drag sidebar icons to reorder
Hidden sections accessible via command palette
Reset sidebar to defaults
Newly installed plugin appears at end of sidebar
Bottom-pinned items always remain at bottom
Sidebar state survives app restart

phases.name: norbert-session Migration (M3)
phases.description: Migrate Phase 2 session list into first-party plugin. Validate API sufficiency across all placement targets.

steps.name: norbert-session plugin entry and view registration
steps.description: Implement norbert-session as standalone plugin using only public NorbertAPI. Register Session List and Session Detail views, sidebar tab, hook processor. Uses zero internal Norbert APIs.
acceptance_criteria: norbert-session registers views via standard plugin API
Plugin uses only public NorbertPlugin/NorbertAPI interface
norbert-session loads via standard plugin loader like any plugin
Sessions sidebar icon appears and clicking assigns to Main zone
scenarios: norbert-session registers views via plugin API
norbert-session is a standalone plugin using only public API
norbert-session loads via plugin loader like any plugin

steps.name: Walking skeleton: norbert-session across all placement targets
steps.description: Validate Session List works in Main zone, Secondary zone, floating panel with live pill metric, and new window with live updates. Verify all assignment mechanisms. Persist placement across restart.
acceptance_criteria: Session List assignable to Main, Secondary, floating panel, and new window
Floating panel pill shows live active session count
Session List in new window receives live updates
All Phase 2 session list functionality preserved (no regression)
Session List placement persists in layout file across restart
scenarios: norbert-session works across all placement targets (walking skeleton)
Session List view persists in layout across restart
Session List in floating panel shows live metric
Session List in new window receives live updates
All Phase 2 session list functionality preserved
norbert-session hook processor receives session events
Session List placement persists in layout file

steps.name: App.tsx refactor and legacy view removal
steps.description: Remove hardcoded session views from App.tsx. Layout Engine + plugin view rendering replaces monolithic layout. Existing domain modules remain shared.
acceptance_criteria: App.tsx delegates view rendering to Layout Engine
No hardcoded view imports remain in core app shell
Domain types (status.ts, eventDetail.ts) remain accessible to plugins
scenarios: All Phase 2 session list functionality preserved
